fact: February 6 is exactly 37 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 10% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are typically still fast asleep underground right now, dreaming through their winter naps that can last up to 150 days!
February 6 falls in the middle of the coldest stretch of winter in many northern regions, when the air is so cold it can freeze your breath instantly!
Tree sap is usually frozen solid right now in cold regions - like nature's own popsicles running through every branch and trunk!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier today than it did just one week ago in northern climates, and you can actually notice the difference if you pay attention!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, February 6 is peak summer heat - some places often hit their hottest temperatures of the entire year around now!
Polar bears in the Arctic are typically having babies right about now, giving birth to cubs the size of hamsters in cozy snow dens!
We're still 44 days away from the spring equinox, which means winter has a long way to go in the north - but every day gets a tiny bit longer!
February 6 falls in the 6th week of the year, making it a double-six day!
Penguins in Antarctica are usually busy feeding their fluffy gray chicks right now, bringing up fishy meals directly into their babies' mouths!
The moon phase around February 6 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, the night sky looks extra bright bouncing off winter snow in northern regions!
